title: Stating a compass bearing
problem: A direction is $55°$ measured from north toward the east. Write this direction as a compass bearing.

steps.working: The direction is measured from north, so the first letter is N.

steps.instruction: Identify which side the direction moves toward.
steps.working: The direction moves toward the east, so the last letter is E.

steps.instruction: Write the compass bearing in N/S–angle–E/W notation.
steps.working: Combining these: the compass bearing is N$55°$E.

title: Converting a compass bearing to a true bearing
problem: Convert the compass bearing S$35°$E to a true bearing.

steps.instruction: Identify the reference pole and determine its true bearing.
steps.working: The reference pole is south, which is $180°$ on the true bearing scale.

steps.instruction: Determine the direction of the angle from the reference pole.
steps.working: S[x]°E means the direction is x° measured from south toward the east. Moving toward east from south means moving anti-clockwise, so the true bearing will be less than $180°$.

steps.instruction: Check the answer is in the correct quadrant.
steps.working: $145°$ lies between $90°$ (east) and $180°$ (south), which is the SE quadrant. A direction east of south should be in the SE quadrant — this is consistent.

title: Converting a compass bearing and identifying the quadrant
problem: A compass bearing of N$72°$W is given. Convert it to a true bearing and state the quadrant in which the direction lies.

steps.instruction: Identify the reference pole and its true bearing value.
steps.working: The reference pole is north, which is $360°$ (equivalently $0°$) on the true bearing scale.

steps.instruction: Determine the direction of the angle from north.
steps.working: N[x]°W means x° measured from north toward the west. Moving from north toward the west is anti-clockwise, so the true bearing will be less than $360°$ but greater than $270°$. Equivalently, subtract x° from $360°$.
$288°$ lies between $270°$ (west) and $360°$ (north). This places the direction in the NW quadrant, which is consistent with a compass bearing that starts with N and ends with W.

answer: The true bearing is $288°$. The direction lies in the NW quadrant.
